title: Rich Crisp Ukrainian Pork Shoulder Stew
description: A hearty one-pot Ukrainian dish featuring pork shoulder, cooked to perfection with a crisp crust and a rich winey flavor, suitable for low-glycemic diets.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the pork shoulder with salt, black pepper, and caraway seeds.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the pork shoulder until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Remove the pork from the pot and set it aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until it is softened and translucent.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Add the dry white wine and chicken broth to the pot, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom.
Return the pork shoulder to the pot and bring the liquid to a simmer.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the pork for 2 1/2 hours, or until it is tender and easily shreds with a fork.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Use a sous-vide machine to crisp the pork shoulder at 160 degrees Fahrenheit for 1 hour.
